REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS AND FOR STATUTORY LEGAL COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART. THE LIS PENDENS IS EXPUNGED. PLAINTIFF SCOTT MAINES FAILED TO ALLEGE AND SHOW THAT HE HAS A REASONABLE PROBABILITY OF PROVING A REAL PROPERTY CLAIM AGAINST MS. WIGET. MS. WIGET IS AWARDED REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS OF $8,301.40, CONSISTING OF 20 HOURS OF REASONABLE ATTORNEY TIME AT THE REASONABLE RATE OF $400 PER HOUR PLUS $301.50 IN COSTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Statutory Legal Costs
Off Calendar Jul-15-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Vasko Alexander ; 949-421-3030; LS Carlson Law, PC; appearing via Zoom for plaintiff.
James Cuadra; 415-426-3502; Quadra & Coll, LLP; appearing via Zoom for defendant, Pamel Wiget.

Mr. Alexander contests the courts tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PAMELA WIGET , AN INDIVDUAL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Statutory Legal Costs is granted in part. The lis pendens is expunged. Plaintiff Scott Maines failed to allege and show that he has a reasonable probability of proving a real property claim against Ms. Wiget. Ms. Wiget is awarded reasonable fees and costs of $8,301.40, consisting of 20 hours of reasonable attorney time at the reasonable rate of $400 per hour plus $301.50 in costs.

Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Richard Canada Esq., LS Carlson Law, 949-421-3030 via zoom for plaintiff
James Quadra Esq., Quadra & Coll, LLP, 415-426-3502, via zoom special apperance for defendant, Pamela Wiget
John Gill Esq., Hughes Gill Cochrane Tinetti, P.C., 925-926-1200, via zoom special apperance for defendant, 2195 Green Street Condominium Association

Mr. Canada contests the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is DENIED. No showing of irreparable harm at this time.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
